title: OpenAI job listing signals publisher ad network ambitions
excerpt: An OpenAI job listing hinted at ambitions to build "publisher-side monetization" tools. OpenAI edited the ad after we asked about it.
content_html:
Exclusive

An OpenAI job listing described ambitions to build an ad network. Then the company deleted the references.

By Lara O'Reilly

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Author of the CMO Insider newsletter

A recent OpenAI job listing hints that the AI giant is laying the groundwork for an ad network.

So far, OpenAI's nascent ad business has been a platform for advertisers looking to reach ChatGPT users. Now, there's evidence that OpenAI might want to jump into a new side of the market — helping publishers and other companies sell their own ad space to marketers, CMO Insider can exclusively report.

The OpenAI job ad, posted last week, called for a "support delivery lead" in its ads division. The job posting, which OpenAI modified after CMO Insider asked about it, referenced building a publisher-facing ad business, the first time the company has publicly mentioned ambitions in the area.

Such a move could boost ad revenue, offer marketers placements beyond the ChatGPT app, and potentially soothe tensions with some publishers.

"This is a huge sign that OpenAI is building toward an advertising business far beyond just ChatGPT," Debra Aho Williamson, founder of the AI-focused research and advisory firm Sonata Insights, told me.

The job posting originally said that the role's publisher-side responsibilities would include "inventory setup, ad serving integrations, reporting, and yield-impacting issues."

This person would "design and continuously improve support coverage for both ad buyers and publishers, ensuring the team can diagnose buyer-side delivery issues and publisher-side monetization and integration issues with equal rigor," the job posting said.

OpenAI updated the job ad after CMO Insider contacted the company about it, removing references to publishers. The company deleted or edited five sentences in the posting. OpenAI later said the role's responsibilities cover agencies and brands.

Intriguing!

So, what could an OpenAI ad network look like?

The job ad didn't fully sketch out what OpenAI is interested in building. The most obvious move for OpenAI would be to enter the world of "demand extension" or "audience extension." OpenAI could take growing advertiser demand and extend those campaigns to placements on a network of external websites or apps. Publishers would be able to monetize their own traffic using the commercial signals gathered by ChatGPT. Think of it as an AI-native equivalent to the Facebook Audience Network or Google Display Network.

OpenAI told CMO Insider it isn't entering demand extension.

Strategically, creating an ad network would make sense for OpenAI, though it would represent an unusually fast jump into supply-side adtech.

One of the biggest advertiser gripes with OpenAI's ad product so far is that advertiser demand has outpaced available supply. Advertisers previously complained that OpenAI had been slow to actually spend their budgets, despite asking for hefty upfront commitments to participate in early ad trials.

OpenAI's head of global ad solutions, Dave Dugan, told me in a June interview that ad delivery and volume had improved as its confidence in the ChatGPT Ads user experience had grown. Still, OpenAI only serves ads to users on ChatGPT's free or lower-priced tiers in select regions, meaning its native ad real estate remains fundamentally constrained by the chat interface.

A publisher ad network could help with the inventory bottleneck.

Building bridges with publishers

Launching a sell-side product would also mark a dramatic shift in OpenAI's relationship with media companies.

To date, publishers do not receive a cut of ChatGPT's ad revenue. Instead, some publishers make money from OpenAI through direct licensing deals for training data and content retrieval. (Business Insider parent company, Axel Springer, has one of these deals.)

The broader relationship remains tense: publishers including The New York Times, Ziff Davis, and a coalition of local news outlets are actively suing OpenAI, alleging copyright infringement. OpenAI has argued that its training of AI models is protected by the fair use doctrine.

An ad network would introduce a new way for OpenAI to compensate publishers, giving them a cut of ad revenue if a user views or clicks an ad served on their site or app.

As OpenAI prepares for an eventual public offering, it faces pressure to scale high-margin revenue streams, such as ads. The company must justify its eye-popping private valuation and offset hundreds of billions in long-term compute and infrastructure commitments.

In April, Axios reported that OpenAI was forecasting about $2.5 billion in ad revenue in 2026 and around $100 billion in 2030.

Nate Elliott, an analyst at BI's sister company EMARKETER, said that for OpenAI to hit those numbers, it would need to dramatically expand its user base, charge top-tier ad rates, and potentially risk ruining the user experience with a heavy ad load.

Or, it could build an ad network.

Read next

Lara O'Reilly

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Lara O'Reilly is the anchor of the CMO Insider newsletter.She is a senior correspondent who has covered the digital advertising, marketing, and media industries since 2010. Her current beat includes big tech companies like Alphabet, and Meta, and adtech firms, agencies,  publishers, the creator economy, and CMOs.Lara has previously worked as a reporter and executive producer at titles including The Wall Street Journal, Digiday, Yahoo Finance, and Marketing Week. She was previously Business Insider's senior global advertising editor from 2014 to 2017.Lara was named "Digital Journalist of the Year" by the London Press Club in 2016.Lara is a regular guest on TV and radio and has appeared on outlets such as the BBC, NPR, SiriusXM's Wharton Business Daily, and CTV Television Network. She also frequently speaks on stage at major events such as Web Summit, IFA, VivaTech, Advertising Week, and Cannes Lions.To get in touch with Lara O'Reilly, email loreilly@businessinsider.com or contact her on Signal at @loreilly.71Check out Insider's source guide for tips on sharing information securely.Read some of Lara's recent work below:

  • Meet Cindy Rose, the former lawyer and top Microsoft exec set to become CEO of ad giant WPP
  • How X CEO Linda Yaccarino went from Elon Musk's fixer to out of a job in 2 years
  • Inside the political reckoning shaking up the ad industry
  • Meet the 'reclusive' tech billionaire making an audacious bid to buy TikTok
  • Top marketers are under a ton of pressure. They told me how they're trying to make themselves recession-proof.
  • Big Tech workers got too used to perks. The pampering is over.

  • OpenAI
  • ChatGPT

blocks.html_en: In April, Axios reported that OpenAI was forecasting about $2.5 billion in ad revenue in 2026 and around $100 billion in 2030.

sentences.en: In April, Axios reported that OpenAI was forecasting about $2.5 billion in ad revenue in 2026 and around $100 billion in 2030.
sentences.zh: 4 月份，Axios 报告称，OpenAI 预计 2026 年广告收入约为 25 亿美元，2030 年约为 1000 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: Nate Elliott, an analyst at BI's sister company EMARKETER, said that for OpenAI to hit those numbers, it would need to dramatically expand its user base, charge top-tier ad rates, and potentially risk ruining the user experience with a heavy ad load.

sentences.en: Nate Elliott, an analyst at BI's sister company EMARKETER, said that for OpenAI to hit those numbers, it would need to dramatically expand its user base, charge top-tier ad rates, and potentially risk ruining the user experience with a heavy ad load.
sentences.zh: BI 姊妹公司 EMARKETER 的分析师 Nate Elliott 表示，OpenAI 要达到这些数字，需要大幅扩大用户群、收取顶级广告费率，并且可能面临因广告负载过重而破坏用户体验的风险。

blocks.html_en: Or, it could build an ad network.

sentences.en: Or, it could build an ad network.
sentences.zh: 或者，它可以建立一个广告网络。

blocks.html_en: Lara O'Reilly is the anchor of the CMO Insider newsletter.She is a senior correspondent who has covered the digital advertising, marketing, and media industries since 2010. Her current beat includes big tech companies like Alphabet, and Meta, and adtech firms, agencies,  publishers, the creator economy, and CMOs.Lara has previously worked as a reporter and executive producer at titles including The Wall Street Journal, Digiday, Yahoo Finance, and Marketing Week. She was previously Business Insider's senior global advertising editor from 2014 to 2017.Lara was named "Digital Journalist of the Year" by the London Press Club in 2016.Lara is a regular guest on TV and radio and has appeared on outlets such as the BBC, NPR, SiriusXM's Wharton Business Daily, and CTV Television Network. She also frequently speaks on stage at major events such as Web Summit, IFA, VivaTech, Advertising Week, and Cannes Lions.To get in touch with Lara O'Reilly, email loreilly@businessinsider.com or contact her on Signal at @loreilly.71Check out Insider's source guide for tips on sharing information securely.Read some of Lara's recent work below:

content_html_zh:
独家的

OpenAI 的一份职位清单描述了建立广告网络的雄心。 然后该公司删除了这些引用。

作者：Lara O'Reilly 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。 CMO Insider 时事通讯的作者

最近的 OpenAI 职位列表暗示，这家人工智能巨头正在为广告网络奠定基础。

到目前为止，OpenAI 的新兴广告业务已成为希望接触 ChatGPT 用户的广告商的平台。 据 CMO Insider 独家报道，现在有证据表明 OpenAI 可能希望进入市场的新领域——帮助出版商和其他公司向营销人员出售自己的广告空间。

OpenAI 上周发布的招聘广告呼吁在其广告部门设立“支持交付主管”。 OpenAI 在 CMO Insider 询问后修改了该招聘启事，提到建立面向出版商的广告业务，这是该公司首次公开提及该领域的雄心。

此举可以增加广告收入，为营销人员提供 ChatGPT 应用程序之外的展示位置，并有可能缓解与一些出版商的紧张关系。

专注于人工智能的研究和咨询公司 Sonata Insights 的创始人黛布拉·阿霍·威廉姆森 (Debra Aho Williamson) 告诉我：“这是一个巨大的迹象，表明 OpenAI 正在朝着超越 ChatGPT 的方向发展广告业务。”

招聘启事最初表示，该职位的发布商职责包括“库存设置、广告服务集成、报告和影响收益的问题”。

招聘启事称，此人将“设计并不断改善对广告买家和发布商的支持范围，确保团队能够以同样严格的方式诊断买家方交付问题以及发布商方货币化和集成问题”。

在 CMO Insider 联系该公司后，OpenAI 更新了招聘广告，删除了对出版商的提及。 该公司删除或编辑了帖子中的五句话。 OpenAI 随后表示，该职位的职责涵盖代理机构和品牌。

有趣！

那么，OpenAI 广告网络会是什么样子呢？

该招聘广告没有完全勾勒出 OpenAI 有兴趣构建的内容。 OpenAI 最明显的举措是进入“需求延伸”或“受众延伸”的世界。 OpenAI 可以满足不断增长的广告商需求，并将这些活动扩展到外部网站或应用程序网络上的展示位置。 发布商将能够使用 ChatGPT 收集的商业信号将自己的流量货币化。 将其视为相当于 Facebook Audience Network 或 Google Display Network 的 AI 原生产品。

OpenAI 告诉 CMO Insider，它不会进入需求扩展阶段。

从战略上讲，创建一个广告网络对于 OpenAI 来说是有意义的，尽管它代表着向供应方广告技术的异常快速跳跃。

迄今为止，广告商对 OpenAI 广告产品最大的抱怨之一是广告商的需求超过了可用的供应。 广告商此前抱怨 OpenAI 在实际支出预算方面进展缓慢，尽管他们要求预先承诺大量参与早期广告试验。

OpenAI 的全球广告解决方案主管 Dave Dugan 在 6 月份的一次采访中告诉我，随着其对 ChatGPT 广告用户体验的信心增强，广告投放和数量有所改善。 尽管如此，OpenAI 仅在部分地区向 ChatGPT 免费或低价级别的用户提供广告，这意味着其原生广告空间仍然受到聊天界面的根本限制。

发布商广告网络可以帮助解决库存瓶颈。

与出版商建立桥梁

推出卖方产品也标志着 OpenAI 与媒体公司关系的巨大转变。

迄今为止，发布商并未从 ChatGPT 的广告收入中获得分成。 相反，一些出版商通过培训数据和内容检索的直接许可交易从 OpenAI 中赚钱。 （《商业内幕》母公司 Axel Springer 拥有其中一项交易。）

更广泛的关系仍然紧张：包括《纽约时报》、Ziff Davis 在内的出版商以及当地新闻媒体联盟正在积极起诉 OpenAI，指控其侵犯版权。 OpenAI 辩称，其人工智能模型的训练受到合理使用原则的保护。

广告网络将为 OpenAI 引入一种新的方式来补偿发布商，如果用户查看或点击其网站或应用程序上投放的广告，他们就可以从广告收入中分成。

在 OpenAI 为最终的公开募股做准备时，它面临着扩大广告等高利润收入来源的压力。 该公司必须证明其令人瞠目结舌的私人估值的合理性，并抵消数千亿美元的长期计算和基础设施承诺。

4 月份，Axios 报告称，OpenAI 预计 2026 年广告收入约为 25 亿美元，2030 年约为 1000 亿美元。

BI 姊妹公司 EMARKETER 的分析师 Nate Elliott 表示，OpenAI 要达到这些数字，需要大幅扩大用户群、收取顶级广告费率，并且可能面临因广告负载过重而破坏用户体验的风险。

或者，它可以建立一个广告网络。

Lara O'Reilly 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

Lara O'Reilly 是 CMO Insider 时事通讯的主播。她是一名高级记者，自 2010 年以来一直报道数字广告、营销和媒体行业。 她目前的工作范围包括 Alphabet、Meta 等大型科技公司，以及广告科技公司、代理机构、出版商、创作者经济和 CMO。Lara 此前曾在《华尔街日报》、《Digiday》、《雅虎财经》和《营销周刊》等杂志担任记者和执行制片人。 她曾于 2014 年至 2017 年担任 Business Insider 的全球高级广告编辑。Lara 于 2016 年被伦敦新闻俱乐部评为“年度数字记者”。Lara 是电视和广播电台的常客，并出现在 BBC、NPR、SiriusXM 的沃顿商业日报和 CTV 电视网等媒体上。 她还经常在 Web Summit、IFA、VivaTech、Advertising Week 和 Cannes Lions 等重大活动的舞台上发表演讲。要与 Lara O'Reilly 取得联系，请发送电子邮件至 loreilly@businessinsider.com 或通过 Signal 与她联系：@loreilly.71查看 Insider 的源指南，了解有关安全共享信息的提示。请阅读以下 Lara 最近的一些作品：

  • X 首席执行官琳达·雅卡里诺 (Linda Yaccarino) 如何在 2 年内从埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 的修理工变成失业者
